notes: NuTCRACKER v1.0 1994; v3.1 May 1997; continued under MKS; eventually superseded by containerization and cloud migration
NT evolved into Windows 2000 and Windows Server line; NT kernel is basis of all modern Windows
Win32 still exists but modern Windows development uses .NET and UWP; Win32 legacy maintained
POSIX standard still important; NT's POSIX subsystem deprecated; WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux) is modern equivalent
DirectLink enabled Unix RDBMS library access on NT; discontinued with NuTCRACKER under PTC
Unix RISC workstations displaced by x86 Windows and Linux workstations by mid-2000s
MKS Toolkit provided Unix shell and utilities on Windows; included with NuTCRACKER; now legacy
X Windows/Motif was standard Unix GUI; Wintif in NuTCRACKER converted Motif to native Windows UI
Pentium processors cited as NuTCRACKER target; x86 architecture became dominant through x86-64
DCOM supported by NuTCRACKER DirectLink; DCOM evolved into .NET Remoting then WCF then REST
Cross-platform porting evolved into containerization (Docker) and cloud-native development
Tuxedo libraries supported via DirectLink; Oracle Tuxedo still active in legacy environments
